title: Mari Magdalene
wikitext:
[]: "Mari" Magdalene?             Quote Post by Chroma » Sat Oct 13, 2012 11:01 am We all know that Anno just loves including tons and tons of Christian/Jewish/Kabbalah symbolism, usually for no reason, to add to the mysticism and visualizations and ideas of Evangelion (new ones in Rebuild are Bethany Base and the Key of Nebuchadnezzar). And while I'm not religious, I do like researching religions because they can be really interesting. Recently I found out about The Gospel of Mary, a non-canon book of the Bible written by the Gnostics and excluded from the Bible by the Church. In it, Mary Magdalene was one of Jesus' most important disciples. Mary is also seen by many scholars to be the same person as Mary of Bethany. Town of Bethany = Bethany Base Mary = Mari Do you think Anno did this on purpose? I could totally see him adding this as just another layer of his symbolism. And it kinda meshes with my own little side theory that the girls of EVA all stand for something from the Bible (in NGE: Misato = Father (her problem) Asuka = Son (her mother problem, though it's a stretch) and Rei = Holy Spirit. She's supernatural and stuff). Of course, that's just my interpretation and of course it doesn't mean anything but just some correlations I thought about while watching. This probably looks kinda crazy and that I'm reading into things to much, but you guys are here on evageeks still analyzing a 10+ year old show just like I am. And Anno makes it so easy with his meaningless (but delicious) symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Sat Oct 13, 2012 1:22 pm Forgive me for sounding prudish, but Mary Magdalene was a prostitute. Mari is (at the moment) a source of fanservice. Coincidence, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 13, 2012 1:22 pm There's actually some scattered discussion about this if you search for "mari" and "Magdalene" in the search function. No thread for it though.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Sat Oct 13, 2012 1:27 pm driftking18594 wrote: Forgive me for sounding prudish, but Mary Magdalene was a prostitute. Mari is (at the moment) a source of fanservice. Coincidence, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Quadhonk » Sat Oct 13, 2012 1:51 pm driftking18594 wrote: Forgive me for sounding prudish, but Mary Magdalene was a prostitute. Mari is (at the moment) a source of fanservice. Coincidence, right?

[]:             Quote Post by sedemihcrA » Sat Oct 13, 2012 2:26 pm driftking18594 wrote: Forgive me for sounding prudish, but Mary Magdalene was a prostitute.

[]: Re: "Mari" Magdalene?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 13, 2012 2:55 pm Chroma wrote: In it, Mary Magdalene was one of Jesus' most important disciples.

[]:             Quote Post by sedemihcrA » Sat Oct 13, 2012 3:35 pm Haha, Tines! YES! I'm telling you guys.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Oct 13, 2012 3:44 pm The true twist in FINAL is that Mari represents Mary, Mother of Jesus. Everyone's just too busy fondling over Yui's involvement to realize it at this point. But on a more serious note, Mari also has a fetish for that/those which smells of LCL. The way she climbed over Shinji in 2.xx seemed to suggest that she would have reacted that way to anything that smelled of LCL. Seems a bit whorish if you ask me. Though, since Jesus actually tying the knot with Mary was never canon in any of the religious source materials Anno has been using, I don't know if Shinji and Mari being as important to each other's live as say, Shinji and Asuka in The End of Evangelion, will be an element in Redo or FINAL. I guess we'll have to wait and see.

[]: Re: "Mari" Magdalene?             Quote Post by Chroma » Mon Oct 15, 2012 7:19 pm Mr. Tines wrote: She is also the number one candidate for being Jesus' wife. So perhaps she really is the Moyoco figure, and Shinji/Mari will be the NME OTP.

[]: Re: "Mari" Magdalene?             Quote Post by StratoSakuya...AF » Mon Oct 15, 2012 8:28 pm Mr. Tines wrote: She is also the number one candidate for being Jesus' wife. So perhaps she really is the Moyoco figure, and Shinji/Mari will be the NME OTP.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Tue Oct 16, 2012 11:18 am Anno already chose not to use the Mari/Shinji kiss proposal.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Oct 16, 2012 11:31 am I think I previously mentioned that "bethany" connection here It might be a mere pun based on a similar name, or really indicate her future role as confidante of "the chosen one" (=Shinji... their interactions were brief, but it seems like they made an impression on each other, with Mari taking an "advisor-like" role. ), or as a means to characterize Mari - in the story where "Mary of Bethany" appears, she uses her cash to buy some scented oil to wash Jesus' feet, while her sister suggest she use it for something more practical, and she eventually leaves all her stuff behind to follow him, it's a practcial/abstract // heavenly/earthly sort of contrast. The connection to Evangelion's Mari being that she apparently left any daily life behind to accomplish her mission (We practically only see her in pluugsuits, and even when she's not wearing one, she's in uniform.), she is "in touch" with the "heavenly" elements of her world (Beast mode, knowing the EVAs are alive etc... ) Not surprised if Anno makes her wash Shinji's feet more some pointless symbolism... it would probably still look epic, and I can imagine Mari to be the type to make a fuss about feet. It's probably not much more than a reference for the cleverer viewers or those nerdy enough to actually google "bethany", there's probably not multiple layers of meaning behind it, since EVA isn't avout Christianity. Note that there is really some debate on where the biblical Jesus picked up that "Mary" person that follows him around - not his mom, the female disciple and apocryphial girlfriend. Some speculate that she's the whore he saved from stoning, others say that she's the chick who's named as "Mary of Bethany" in an earlier story. There's either conflicting origin stories, or the name was simply just as obnoxiously overused back then as it is now, it wasn't necessarily the same chick. (I really despise the Name Mary/Maria/Marie/etc. I don't care if the mother of a religious figure was called that. If she had happened to have some ridiculous embarassing name like "Ingeborg" or "Brünhilda", would all those zillions girls be called that instead? If God exists, he probably chose her for other criteria than "Which name do I want to plaster on all my followers". And what are you really telling your child with that? "I want you to have a mediocre life like the other X-trillion Marias?" or is it "My religion is more important to me than my kids identity, so prepare to be disowned if you convert to another one or become an atheist". Plus, the meaning is, loosely praphrased, either "baby machine" or "hysterical bitch." Talk about misogynism. There isn't a school class in Europe that has less than one Maria in it! Usually more. And the teachers always go "Which Maria?" Doesn't anyone think that will damage the kid's self-image? Who the heck gives their child the most overused name in the world! Or maybe I'm just pissed because my parents were too lazy to come up with a name for each of their daughters. I'm "Monika Maria" , and I have yet another sister called "Maria". The NEXT YOUNGER sister, to boot. My father would probably have called all five of us Maria (including the lone boy) if mom hadn't stopped him... Seriously, his brother actually did it and named all five of his children after himself. José junior, José-Luis etc... and the only girl, Maria-José. No seriously Maria-José. I've never met that unfortunate cousin of mine thanks to Communism, but I'd bet my computer that her classmates point and laugh at the poor girl. Er... sorry, ranting. ) Anno already chose not to use the Mari/Shinji kiss proposal.

And while I'm not religious, I do like researching religions because they can be r

And it kinda meshes with my own little side theory that the girls of EVA all stand for something from the Bible (in NGE: Misato = Father (her problem) Asuka = Son (her mother problem, though it's a stretch) and Rei = Holy Spirit. And Anno makes it so easy with his meaningless (but delicious) symbolism.
